tables.description: This sample attributes table contains sample IDs, subject IDs, histological type, IPS (Induced Pluripotent Stem Cell ) passage number, antibody markers, sample source, sequencing information (n=9 variables: analyte type, RNA integrity number, library prep kit, fragment size, sequencing type, strand, sequencing cycle number, sequencing platform, sequencing center which conducted sequencing), tumor status, days cells were differentiated into motor neurons, protocol, and cell markers .

variables.name: ANALYTE_TYPE
variables.description: The analyte type of the sample (ex. DNA and RNA)

variables.name: CELL_MARKERS
variables.description: Antibody markers used to classify induced Pluripotent Stem Cell lines or motor neuron cell lines

variables.name: FRAGMENT_SIZE
variables.description: The size of the actual fragments submitted for sequencing after library size-selection.

variables.name: HISTOLOGICAL_TYPE
variables.description: Cell type samples were made from

variables.name: IMN_DAYS_DIFFERENTIATION
variables.description: How many days cells were differentiated into motor neurons

variables.name: IPS_PASSAGE_NUMBER
variables.description: Induced Pluripotent Stem Cell line Passage number used for assay

variables.name: IS_TUMOR
variables.description: The tumor status of the sample

variables.name: LIBRARY_PREP_KIT
variables.description: Kit used to prepare the library for sequencing

variables.name: PROTOCOL
variables.description: Protocol used by Cedars-Sinai Stem Cell Core Facility

variables.name: RIN_VALUE
variables.description: RNA integrity number

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: De-identified Sample ID

variables.name: SAMPLE_SOURCE
variables.description: Source Institution where samples originated

variables.name: SEQUENCING_CENTER
variables.description: Name of the center which conducted sequencing

variables.name: SEQ_CYCLE_NUMBER
variables.description: Number of sequencing cycles used

variables.name: SEQ_PLATFORM
variables.description: Company and instrument model used for sequencing

variables.name: SEQ_TYPE
variables.description: Defines the sequencing strategy. Paired-end sequences inserts in both directions, single-end sequences from one direction

variables.name: STRAND
variables.description: Defines if a particular strand is sequenced

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: AGE
variables.description: Subject age at sampling

variables.name: DIAGNOSIS
variables.description: Clinical diagnosis

variables.name: DIAGNOSIS_DETAIL
variables.description: Gene mutated or subtype of disease diagnosis

variables.name: RACE
variables.description: Race of participant

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Gender of participant

variables.name: SOURCE
variables.description: Institution or repository from where the cell linewas obtained

variables.name: SOURCE_ID
variables.description: ID of source line

variables.name: SOURCE_TISSUE
variables.description: Type of tissue

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: De-identified Subject ID

tables.description: The subject consent file includes subject IDs, consent information, subject aliases, and case control status of the subject for Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS) and Spinal Muscular Atrophy (SMA) disease.

variables.name: AFFECTION_STATUS
variables.description: Case control status of the subject for ALS or SMA disease

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

variables.name: SOURCE_SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID used in the Source Repository

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_SOURCE
variables.description: Source repository where subjects originate

tables.description: This data table contains a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs. The data table also includes sample use and sample aliases.

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: SAMPLE_SOURCE
variables.description: Source of iPS cell line

variables.name: SAMPLE_USE
variables.description: Sample Use

variables.name: SOURCE_SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID used in center generating iPSC lines

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: IPS cell line name
